paper_assessments.experimental_system: Non-human; non-immune system (rat Schwann cells, peripheral nervous system)
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: Egr2 antisense RNA modulates chromatin accessibility of Schwann cells.
paper_assessments.rationale: Concerns Egr2-AS regulation of Schwann cell chromatin in nerve regeneration; no T cell or TCR content whatsoever.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: Non-human/in vitro and in vivo (mouse) primary T cells; anergic CD4 T cells
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: Egr2 deletion prevents anergy induction through diacylglycerol kinase α and restores Ras/MAPK signaling in T cells.
paper_assessments.rationale: Genetic deletion (perturbation) prevents anergy and restores proximal Ras/MAPK signaling via DGKα, a direct causal mechanistic readout of TCR-linked hyporesponsiveness.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: Non-human; non-immune/CNS (rat spinal microglia, neuropathic pain)
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: EGR2 was found to promote microglial phagocytosis, thereby maintaining neuropathic pain.
paper_assessments.rationale: Studies EGR2 in microglial phagocytosis and neuropathic pain; no T cell or TCR relevance.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: Non-human (mouse) primary CD4 and CD8 effector T cells
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: Egr2 and 3 physically interact with the T-box domain of T-bet, blocking T-bet DNA binding.
paper_assessments.rationale: Direct mechanistic/perturbational evidence that Egr2 suppresses T-bet-driven IFN-γ and Th1 differentiation, an effector-restraining phenotype but not proximal TCR-signalling inhibition.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: EGR2 deficiency impairs phagocytosis and antifungal immunity in alveolar macrophages.
paper_assessments.rationale: Addresses EGR2 in macrophage identity and antifungal phagocytosis; no T cell or TCR signalling content.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: Egr2 and 3 are important for the control of inflammatory cytokine production by tolerant T cells.
paper_assessments.rationale: Perturbational study in tolerant T cells, but Egr2/3 control inflammatory cytokines rather than tolerance induction itself; connection to proximal TCR inhibition is incomplete.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: The LPXN/HDAC6/EGR2 axis promotes breast cancer progression by enhancing M2 macrophage polarization.
paper_assessments.rationale: Concerns EGR2 in macrophage M2 polarization and breast cancer; no T cell or TCR inhibition relevance.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: Non-human (mouse) primary CD4 T cells; in vivo immune responses
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: T cells deficient in EGR-2 are hyperproliferative to IL-2 and produce elevated levels of IFN-γ.
paper_assessments.rationale: Knockout study relevant to anergy/tolerance, but reports normal acute responses in vivo, tempering directness; links EGR2 to chronic-antigen hyporesponsiveness rather than proximal TCR inhibition.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: Loss of uterine EGR2 caused mild subfertility in female mice.
paper_assessments.rationale: Examines uterine EGR2 in fertility; no T cell or TCR content.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: TCR-induced Egr2 and Egr1 proteins had elevated and prolonged expression in NKT lineage precursors.
paper_assessments.rationale: Egr2 acts downstream of TCR signalling to instruct NKT lineage differentiation; this is lineage specification, not TCR inhibition or an inhibitory phenotype.

parsed.tier_rationale: PMC3501351 is a primary experimental study providing direct perturbational evidence that Egr2 deletion prevents T cell anergy induction (a TCR-linked hyporesponsive state) and restores proximal Ras/MAPK signalling via DGKα, satisfying the requirement for direct causal evidence with a mechanistic readout on TCR-linked hyporesponsiveness. It is corroborated by additional primary Egr2/3 studies in tolerant/effector T cells (PMC5946152, PMC5439026, PMC2944839), consistently showing Egr2 promotes inhibitory/tolerogenic T cell control.
